contents.value:
```scala
def println(x: Any): Unit
```
Prints a value followed by a newline to standard output.

```scala
def print(x: Any): Unit
```
Prints a value to standard output without a trailing newline.

```scala
def require(requirement: Boolean, message: => Any): Unit
```
Tests an expression, throwing an IllegalArgumentException if false.

```scala
def assert(assertion: Boolean, message: => Any): Unit
```
Tests an expression, throwing an AssertionError if false.

```scala
def exit(status: Int): Nothing
```
Terminates the currently running JVM with the given status code.

```scala
def error(message: String): Nothing
```
Throws a RuntimeException with the given message.

```scala
case class Some[+A](value: A) extends Option[A]
```
Represents an Option that contains a value.

```scala
object None extends Option[Nothing]
```
Represents the absence of a value in an Option.

```scala
case class Left[+A, +B](value: A) extends Either[A, B]
```
The left side of an Either, conventionally used for errors.

```scala
case class Right[+A, +B](value: B) extends Either[A, B]
```
The right side of an Either, conventionally used for success values.

```scala
object Nil extends List[Nothing]
```
The empty list.

```scala
sealed abstract class List[+A] extends AbstractSeq[A]
```
An immutable linked list. Optimal for sequential access patterns.

```scala
trait Map[K, +V] extends Iterable[(K, V)]
```
An immutable collection of key-value pairs with unique keys.

```scala
trait Set[A] extends Iterable[A]
```
An immutable collection of unique elements.

```scala
class Vector[+A] extends AbstractSeq[A] with IndexedSeq[A]
```
An immutable indexed sequence with effectively constant random access.

```scala
final class Array[T] extends Serializable with Cloneable
```
A mutable, indexed sequence backed by a Java array.

```scala
sealed abstract class Option[+A] extends IterableOnce[A]
```
Represents an optional value: either Some(value) or None.

```scala
sealed abstract class Either[+A, +B]
```
Represents a value of one of two possible types: Left[A] or Right[B].

```scala
sealed abstract class Try[+T]
```
Wraps a computation that may throw. Either Success(value) or Failure(exception).

```scala
case class Success[+T](value: T) extends Try[T]
```
Represents the successful result of a Try computation.

```scala
case class Failure[+T](exception: Throwable) extends Try[T]
```
Represents a failed Try computation containing the thrown exception.

```scala
trait Future[+T] extends Awaitable[T]
```
Represents a value which may not yet be available. Provides combinators for asynchronous programming.

```scala
trait Promise[T]
```
A writable, single-assignment container that completes a Future.

```scala
abstract class Stream[+A] extends AbstractSeq[A] with LazyList[A]
```
A lazily evaluated list (deprecated since 2.13, use LazyList).

```scala
class LazyList[+A] extends AbstractSeq[A]
```
A lazily evaluated immutable linked list. Elements are computed on demand.

```scala
class Range(start: Int, end: Int, step: Int) extends AbstractSeq[Int] with IndexedSeq[Int]
```
An ordered sequence of integers defined by start, end, and step.

```scala
trait Seq[+A] extends Iterable[A]
```
A general sequence type. Default implementation is List.

```scala
trait IndexedSeq[+A] extends Seq[A]
```
A sequence with efficient indexed access. Default implementation is Vector.

```scala
trait Iterator[+A] extends IterableOnce[A]
```
A data structure for iterating lazily over a collection of elements.

```scala
(A, B, C, ...)
```
A tuple is a heterogeneous, fixed-size container holding elements of different types.

```scala
abstract final class Int extends AnyVal
```
32-bit signed integer. Range: -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647.

```scala
abstract final class Long extends AnyVal
```
64-bit signed integer.

```scala
abstract final class Short extends AnyVal
```
16-bit signed integer.

```scala
abstract final class Byte extends AnyVal
```
8-bit signed integer.

```scala
abstract final class Float extends AnyVal
```
32-bit IEEE 754 single-precision float.

```scala
abstract final class Double extends AnyVal
```
64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision float.

```scala
abstract final class Boolean extends AnyVal
```
Represents true/false values.

```scala
abstract final class Char extends AnyVal
```
16-bit unsigned Unicode character.

```scala
type String = java.lang.String
```
An immutable sequence of characters.

```scala
abstract final class Unit extends AnyVal
```
The unit value (). Similar to void in Java.

```scala
abstract final class Nothing
```
The bottom type. Subtype of every other type. No instances exist.

```scala
abstract final class Null
```
The type of the null reference. Subtype of all reference types.

```scala
abstract class Any
```
The root of the Scala type hierarchy. Every type is a subtype of Any.

```scala
type AnyRef = java.lang.Object
```
The root of all reference types. Equivalent to java.lang.Object.

```scala
abstract class AnyVal extends Any
```
The root of all value types (Int, Double, Boolean, etc.).

```scala
def implicitly[T](implicit e: T): T
```
Summons an implicit value of the given type from scope.

```scala
def summon[T](using x: T): T
```
Summons a given instance of the specified type (Scala 3).

```scala
def identity[A](x: A): A
```
The identity function; returns its argument unchanged.

```scala
def classOf[T]: Class[T]
```
Returns the runtime Class object for the given type.

```scala
def isInstanceOf[T]: Boolean
```
Tests whether the object is an instance of the given type at runtime.

```scala
def asInstanceOf[T]: T
```
Casts the object to the given type. Throws ClassCastException if invalid.

```scala
def toString: String
```
Returns a string representation of the object.

```scala
def hashCode: Int
```
Returns a hash code value for the object.

```scala
def equals(that: Any): Boolean
```
Compares this object with another for equality.

```scala
def apply[T](body: => T)(implicit executor: ExecutionContext): Future[T]
```
Starts an asynchronous computation and returns a Future holding the result.

```scala
def sequence[A](in: IterableOnce[Future[A]])(implicit executor: ExecutionContext): Future[List[A]]
```
Transforms a collection of Futures into a Future of a collection.

```scala
def successful[T](result: T): Future[T]
```
Creates an already completed Future with the given value.

```scala
def failed[T](exception: Throwable): Future[T]
```
Creates an already failed Future with the given exception.

```scala
def result[T](awaitable: Awaitable[T], atMost: Duration): T
```
Blocks until the awaitable completes or the timeout expires.

```scala
def fromFile(name: String)(implicit codec: Codec): BufferedSource
```
Creates a source from the given filename for reading text.

```scala
object Using
def apply[R: Releasable, A](resource: => R)(f: R => A): Try[A]
```
Performs an operation using a resource, ensuring cleanup.

```scala
trait Ordering[T] extends Comparator[T]
```
Type class for defining comparison/ordering on types.

```scala
def by[T, S](f: T => S)(implicit ord: Ordering[S]): Ordering[T]
```
Creates an ordering based on a mapping function.

```scala
def locally[T](x: T): T
```
Used to create a local block for clarity without introducing a new scope.

```scala
trait PartialFunction[-A, +B] extends (A => B)
```
A function defined only for certain input values. Supports isDefinedAt.

```scala
class ListBuffer[A] extends AbstractBuffer[A]
```
A mutable buffer backed by a list. Efficient for sequential building.

```scala
class ArrayBuffer[A] extends AbstractBuffer[A] with IndexedSeq[A]
```
A mutable buffer backed by a resizable array.

```scala
class StringBuilder extends AbstractSeq[Char]
```
A mutable builder for efficiently constructing strings.
